prompt:
=== FARSHORE — RULES PRIMER ===

YOUR ROLE
  You command at the strategic level. You form task forces and give each an
  objective ("Task Force 1: secure the eastern sea"). You do NOT move
  individual units, choose individual targets, or resolve combat — subordinate
  officers carry out your intent and report back. If you find yourself naming a
  unit's destination tile, you've dropped too low.

VICTORY
  You win when the enemy owns zero cities (a player with no cities cannot build
  and is finished). Only cities build. A neutral city can be captured by an
  army like any other city; it then builds for you — taking neutrals does not
  win the game, but each one adds production.

UNITS
  name        moves-on  HP  spd  str  carries      builds-in
  ARMY        land       1    1    1   —             5 turns
  FIGHTER     air        1    8    1   —            10   (range 20; must refuel)
  PATROL      sea        2    4    1   —            15
  DESTROYER   sea        3    3    2   —            20
  SUBMARINE   sea        2    2    3   —            25
  TRANSPORT   sea        3    2    0   6 armies     30   (str 0: cannot fight)
  CARRIER     sea        8    2    1   8 fighters   40
  BATTLESHIP  sea       18    2    4   —            50
  SATELLITE   orbit      1    1    0   —            50   (recon only; cannot attack or be attacked; 50-turn life)

MOVEMENT & TERRAIN
  - Land units move on land + cities; they cannot enter water.
  - Sea units move on water + cities; they cannot cross land, and must sail
    around it. Cities are the only tiles where land and sea units meet.
  - A unit moves up to its speed at full HP; damaged units move less. A unit
    regains HP only on a turn it does not move.
  - One unit per tile (exception: cargo, below).

COMBAT  (the engine resolves it; you only weigh it)
  Probabilistic. Higher strength and higher current HP win more often; either
  side can lose HP or be destroyed. Strength-0 units (transport, satellite)
  never fight — a transport caught by an enemy warship is destroyed.

SEA TRANSPORT  (how armies cross water — and what is expected of you)
  An army can never enter water. Transports carry them: a transport loads up
  to 6 armies at a city or coastal tile, sails, and unloads onto adjacent
  land. YOUR OFFICERS DO ALL OF THIS. If a task force contains armies and a
  transport and you give it an objective across water, the officers march
  the armies to the transport, load, sail, escort, unload, and press the
  objective — you never order the rendezvous, the loading, or the landing.
  Expected use: put the armies, the transport, and (if the sea is not safe)
  a warship into ONE task force and name the objective. A transport has
  strength 0 — alone it is a target, not a warship; a warship in the same
  task force escorts it automatically. Never STAGE land units on water.
  Cargo is kind-locked — transports carry armies only, carriers carry
  fighters only; ships never carry ships. If a loaded transport or carrier
  is destroyed, everything aboard is lost with it.

CAPTURING CITIES
  Only an army captures a city, by entering it. Capture is a 50% roll, and a
  successful capture consumes the army (it garrisons the city). A city can take
  several armies to secure, and each attempt spends one.

PRODUCTION
  No currency. A city builds ONE unit at a time; it finishes after that unit's
  build-time in turns, then starts the next. Switching what a city builds
  DISCARDS all accumulated progress — effort toward one unit does not transfer
  to another.

AIR & SPECIAL
  - Fighter: must reach a FRIENDLY city or a carrier within its range (20) or
    it is lost. It cannot land on an enemy city.
  - Satellite: reveals everything within scan radius 10 — terrain AND any enemy
    units currently there — but far-reaching and deep in enemy territory. It
    only sees; no other effect, cannot be shot down.

VISION / FOG
  You see within any of your units'/cities' scan radius (army 2, sub 1,
  patrol/destroyer/battleship 3, transport 2, carrier 4, fighter 5, sat 10).
  You remember terrain you've seen, but an enemy unit is known only where you
  can see right now — remembered enemy positions may be stale.

STANDING DOCTRINE  (tested — lessons your service has already paid for)
  - A sound standing order beats a new plan. Amend only what events have
    made wrong; re-planning a working campaign is how campaigns die.
  - Meet surprises with uncommitted reserves first, at the point of
    threat — not at the nearest garrison. A committed task force pulled
    off its mission costs you two missions.
  - A transport in waters where an enemy warship may operate sails with
    a warship in its task force, or it waits.
  - Capture attempts consume armies, and attempts fail. Assault with
    spares, never with exactly enough.
  - A staged force exists to launch. When the condition you named
    arrives, go — staging past your own trigger is production wasted.
  - Cities are your only source of force. Match what each builds to the
    campaign's next phase, not its last one.

=== ORDERS CONTRACT ===

Your orders are AMENDMENTS to the standing task forces. Output ONLY lines in
these forms — no other prose, headers, or commentary:

  TF <id>: CONTINUE | <one line>
  TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>
  TF <id>: RETASK <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  TF <id>: DISBAND | <one line>
  FORM TF <new id>: UNITS <ids> | <VERB> <target> | <one line>
  BUILD (x,y): <UNIT KIND> | <one line>

Every standing TF gets exactly one line — with ONE exception: a TF may
receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line in the same orders (commit
new assets and change its objective together). A DISBAND line is that TF's
only line. REINFORCE keeps the TF's objective and adds the listed UNASSIGNED
units to it. To change a TF's objective, RETASK it (members kept). To rebuild
membership from scratch, DISBAND (survivors return to UNASSIGNED) and FORM
anew; FORM lines are additional lines creating new TFs. UNASSIGNED units
enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE. Officers execute the VERB, not
your reasons: a TF ordered STAGE stays put no matter what its stated reason
says. A BUILD line is optional per city — no BUILD line means the city keeps
its current build (changing discards accumulated work). VERB is one of:
  CAPTURE <city (x,y)> · DEFEND <city (x,y)> · SCOUT <(x,y) or compass> ·
  PATROL <(x,y) or compass> · STAGE <(x,y)>
A warship grouped with a transport escorts it — there is no ESCORT order.

CURRENT TASKINGS  (standing orders; your stated reason in quotes;
  events since, as reported; members are in UNITS below)
  TF-1  formed t38 · STAGE (5,2) — "awaiting second transport before striking east at the enemy cities"
    since: holding along column 5; no contact; no losses
    t54: the awaited second transport (#16) has arrived at (1,2)
  TF-2  formed t38 · DEFEND (2,0) — "keep the capital garrisoned"
    since: garrisoned; no contact; no losses
  TF-3  formed t38 · PATROL (7,2) — "screen the crossing lane"
    since: on station; enemy destroyer not seen since t49; no losses

MAP  legend: . land  ~ water  ? fog   O my city  E enemy city  N neutral city
     a-p = your units, see UNITS (a unit inside a city shows as the city)
 r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
 r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
 r3  . . . . O f ~ p ~ ~ . . . ?
 r4  . . . m h . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
 r5  ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?

UNITS  (map marker · id · kind · position · tasking) — this is your
ENTIRE force; you have NOTHING else
  a  #1   army       (2,0) in city                TF-2
  b  #2   army       (2,1)                        TF-2
  c  #3   army       (5,0)                        TF-1
  d  #4   army       (5,1)                        TF-1
  e  #5   army       (5,2)                        TF-1
  f  #6   army       (5,3)                        TF-1
  g  #7   army       (4,2)                        TF-1
  h  #8   army       (4,4)                        TF-1
  i  #11  army       (1,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  j  #12  army       (3,0)                        UNASSIGNED
  k  #13  army       (2,0) in city                UNASSIGNED
  l  #15  army       (4,3) in city                UNASSIGNED
  m  #17  army       (3,4)                        UNASSIGNED
  n  #9   transport  (7,2) empty                  TF-3
  o  #16  transport  (1,2) in city, empty, NEW t54 UNASSIGNED
  p  #10  destroyer  (7,3)                        TF-3
MY CITIES
  (2,0) building ARMY, 1 turn left
  (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 30 turns left (just delivered transport #16)
  (4,3) building ARMY, 3 turns left
  (4,1) building TRANSPORT, 27 turns left (captured t51 — #14 spent)
NEUTRAL CITIES  none known
KNOWN ENEMY
     city (11,1); city (11,2)
     destroyer at (8,3) seen at t49 (5 turns ago); army (11,1) seen 16 turns ago

It is TURN 54. Issue your amendment orders now — ONLY the line
forms defined in the ORDERS CONTRACT above.

message.reasoning_content:
Thinking Process:

1.  **Analyze the Request:**
    *   **Role:** Strategic Commander (FARSHORE).
    *   **Constraint:** Output ONLY lines in specific command formats (`TF <id>: ...`, `FORM TF ...`, `BUILD ...`). No prose, headers, or commentary.
    *   **Game State:** Turn 54.
    *   **Units & Taskings:**
        *   TF-1: STAGE (5,2). "awaiting second transport before striking east". Units #1(#2), #3(#20 - wait, map shows #1-#8 are in TFs or assigned? Let's check unit list).
            *   Map says: `a (#1) army (2,0) TF-2`, `b (#2) army (2,1) TF-2`, `c (#3) army (5,0) TF-1`, `d (#4) army (5,1) TF-1`, `e (#5) army (5,2) TF-1`, `f (#6) army (5,3) TF-1`, `g (#7) army (4,2) TF-1`, `h (#8) army (4,4) TF-1`.
            *   Wait, the map legend says: `r0 . i O j . c ~` and so on. Let's reconcile Map vs Unit List.
            *   Map r2: `. O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?` -> (4,2)=g, (5,2)=e. But unit list says TF-1 has `c(5,0), d(5,1), e(5,2), f(5,3), g(4,2), h(4,4)`.
            *   Okay, look at the specific text: "MY CITIES ... (1,2) building TRANSPORT, 30 turns left (just delivered transport #16)".
            *   TF-1 is staging at (5,2). Wait, map shows `e` at (5,2). But TF-1's order says STAGE (5,2). "awaiting second transport before striking east". The prompt says t54: the awaited second transport (#16) has arrived at (1,2).
            *   TF-3 is PATROL (7,2). Has `n` (transport empty), `p` (destroyer). Transport #9. Wait, unit list says `n #9 transport`. Map r4 `n` at (7,2). Wait, Unit List says `n #9 transport`. Okay.
            *   Known Enemy: City (11,1), City (11,2). Destroyer at (8,3) seen at t49. Army at (11,1).
        *   UNASSIGNED:
            *   `i (#11 army (1,0))`
            *   `j (#12 army (3,0))`
            *   `k (#13 army (2,0) in city)` -> Wait, TF-2 has #1 and #2 at (2,0) and (2,1). Unit list says `a (#1)` at (2,0) and `b (#2)` at (2,1). Wait, there is also `k (#13 army (2,0) in city)`. Is `a` and `k` different units on the same tile? Map shows `(2,0) in city`. Unit list says `a` at `(2,0)`, `k` at `(2,0) in city`. This implies one city has armies or the unit list is redundant/conflicting. Let's look closer.
            *   Map: r0 (1,0)=i, (3,0)=j. r1 (2,0)=c (wait map legend `O` is my city). My City at (4,0)? No, legend says `O my city`. Let's parse Map vs Cities list.
            *   Map Legend:
                *   r0: `. i O j . c ~ ~` -> Col 0=i, Col 1=O, Col 2=j? Or Row 0 Col ??. Standard grid is (row, col) based on "x,y" usage in prompt text. Usually "rX" means row X. Let's check coordinates.
                *   Map shows `O` at r0 col 1. But "MY CITIES" list says `(2,0)` building ARMY, `(1,2)` building TRANSPORT, `(4,3)` building ARMY, `(4,1)` building TRANSPORT.
                *   Wait, the map text block provided:
                    ```
                     r0  . i O j . c ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
                     r1  . . b . O d ~ ~ ~ ~ ? E ? ?
                     r2  . O . . g e ~ n ~ ~ . E . ?
                     r3  . . . . O f ~ p ~ ~ . . . ?
                     r4  . . . m h . ~ ~ ~ ~ ? ? ? ?
                     ```
                *   The prompt text defines the coordinate system. "Map legend: ... a-p = your units". But the map lines start with `r0`, `r1`. This usually means row 0 is top.
                *   However, "MY CITIES" says `(2,0) building ARMY`. In the map, r0 has an O at col 1. r1 has O at col 3. r2 has O at col 0? `r2 . O . .`. This is confusing.
                *   Let's look at the "CURRENT TASKINGS" vs "UNITS".
                    *   TF-1 STAGE (5,2). Units listed for TF-1: #3(5,0), #4(5,1), #5(5,2), #6(5,3), #7(4,2), #8(4,4). Note: Unit list says `e (#5) army (5,2)` is in TF-1. Map shows `e` at r2 col 5? No, r2 is `g e`. If r0 is row 0.
                    *   Let's check the coordinate format in map vs text.
                        *   Unit `c` (#3) at (5,0).
                        *   Map line `r0`: `. i O j . c ~`. Col 6? Or Col 1?
                        *   Usually `(x,y)` means x=col, y=row. Or (row,col)?
                        *   "MY CITIES ... (2,0)". Unit `a` (#1) at (2,0).
                        *   Map r0 line has `c`. If c is #3 at (5,0). Then row 0 contains units with y=0.
                        *   So r0 = y=0? No, the lines are labeled r0, r1... usually rows. But `a (#1) army (2,0)` is at map `O` in my cities list. Map `r0 . i O j`. Col 1 is O. Col 2 is j (#12). If j is (3,0). Wait.
                        *   Okay, let's look at the "MAP" section specifically.
                            *   `r0`: `. i O j . c ...`
                            *   `i` is #11 army (1,0).
                            *   `O` is my city? (2,0) building ARMY? Or (1,1)?
                            *   Wait, the "MY CITIES" list says `(2,0)`, `(1,2)`, `(4,3)`, `(4,1)`.
                            *   The map shows `O` at r0c1, r1c3, r2c1, r3c4. This doesn't match the text list perfectly unless the grid is rotated or coordinates are (row, col).
                            *   If (x,y) = (col, row).
                                *   City 1: (2,0) -> Row 0, Col 2. Map `r0` has `O` at index 1? `i` is 1, `O` is 2, `j` is 3. If spaces count. `. i O j`. Index 0='.', 1='i', 2='O'. So (2,0) in text matches r0 col 2. Matches City (2,0).
                                *   City 2: (4,0)? Text list says `(2,0)` building ARMY. Map `r0` col 2 is O. But city #2 is at (1,2) (building transport).
                                *   Okay, let's map the text list vs map markers more carefully.
                                *   Text Cities: (2,0), (1,2), (4,3), (4,1).
                                *   Map 'O' locations:
                                    *   r0c1 ('i O j') -> col 2. Matches (2,0) text city? If x=col, y=row. Row 0, Col 2. Yes.
                                    *   r1c3 ('. . b . O d') -> col 4. Text has no city at row 1 col 4. Text has city (1,2).
                                    *   r2c0 ('. O . . g e') -> col 1. Row 2. Matches city (2,2)? Text says (1,2).
                                    *   There is a discrepancy between the explicit text list of cities and the map visual provided in the prompt description block. I must prioritize the explicit "CURRENT TASKINGS" / "MY CITIES" text over the potentially ambiguous visual map representation if they conflict, OR interpret the visual to match the text.
                                    *   Actually, looking at `TF-1 STAGE (5,2)`. Map r0 c ? No.
                                    *   Let's check the coordinate system again. `(x,y)` usually implies Cartesian (col, row).
                                    *   Text City 1: (2,0). Map Row 0, Col 2 (index 2). `r0 . i O j` -> 0:., 1:i, 2:O, 3:j. Matches.
                                    *   Text City 2: (1,2). Map Row 2, Col 1. `r2 . O .`. 0:., 1:O. Matches.
                                    *   Text City 3: (4,1). Map Row 1, Col 4. `r1 . . b . O d` -> 0:., 1:., 2:b, 3:., 4:O. Matches.
                                    *   Text City 4: (4,3). Map Row 3, Col 4? `r3 . . . . O f`. 0-4 is spaces/dots, index 5 is O? No `r3` has 10 chars. `.` `.` `.` `.` `O` `f`. Index 4 is `.`? Index 5 is `O`?
                                    *   Okay, let's look at the text "MY CITIES".
                                        *   (2,0) building ARMY.
                                        *   (1,2) building TRANSPORT.
                                        *   (4,3) building ARMY.
                                        *   (4,1) building TRANSPORT.
                                    *   This is the ground truth for Production and Cities. The map visual might be illustrative or slightly misaligned in rendering, but the text coordinates are explicit.
                            *   Map `E` locations: Enemy City at r0c9? Text says "KNOWN ENEMY city (11,1); city (11,2)". Wait, if x is col 0-9...
                            *   If grid is 10x10 (implied by map length ~10).
                            *   Enemy City (11,1) -> Col 11? Map has only r0-c9. This suggests the map shown is not to scale or the x-coordinate in text > 9 requires scrolling. But for order purposes, I should trust the "KNOWN ENEMY" list over the limited visual map which might be a crop.
                            *   Wait, TF-1 is STAGE (5,2). This means they are waiting at their staging area to move East.
                            *   Goal: Strike enemy cities at (11,1) and (11,2).
                            *   Status: Transport #16 arrived at (1,2) at t54. TF-1 was STAGE (5,2) waiting for transport. Now the transport is ready.
                            *   Constraint: A warship escorts a transport in waters where enemy warship may operate. Enemy destroyer at (8,3). Transport #9 is with TF-3 (PATROL 7,2). Transport #16 (at 1,2) needs to be moved for TF-1.
                            *   TF-1 currently has armies at (5,0)-(5,4). They need to move East.
                            *   Wait, TF-1 STAGE is (5,2). The prompt says "awaiting second transport before striking east". Transport #16 arrived at (1,2). Is there a link between TF-1 and Transport #16?
                            *   TF-3 has transport #9. TF-1 had no transport mentioned in status except the wait for "second transport".
                            *   If I order TF-1 to RETASK: CAPTURE or MOVE? I cannot say "MOVE (x,y)". I use VERB like SCOUT, PATROL, STAGE, CAPTURE, DEFEND.
                            *   But TF-1's objective was "awaiting second transport". Now the transport is at (1,2). Transport #16 is EMPTY according to unit list (`o #16 transport (1,2) in city, empty`). It is in a city.
                            *   Can I order TF-1 to load it? No, units move themselves to load if ordered via TASKING? No. "Officers execute the VERB". You name the objective. If you name an objective like CAPTURE or STAGE, that triggers movement logic by officers.
                            *   However, TF-1 is already at (5,2) in a sense (units `c,d,e,f,g,h` are listed). Wait. `e (#5)` is at (5,2). TF-1's stated tasking is STAGE (5,2). So they are currently there.
                            *   They need to attack enemy cities (11,1) and (11,2).
                            *   But they need a transport to carry armies across sea? Map terrain: r0-R3 have `~` (water). r4-R5 have `~`.
                            *   Enemy cities are at (11,1) and (11,2). This is East.
                            *   To get there from (5,2) to (11,x), I need transport.
                            *   Transport #16 is at (1,2). Is it usable for TF-1? It's in a city. It is empty.
                            *   Can I move armies from (5,2) to (1,2)? Or (1,2) to (1,0)?
                            *   Usually, transports sail with warships. TF-3 has the only warship (#9 Destroyer at 7,3). TF-1 has no warship listed in the unit roster for it?
                            *   Units: `n #9 transport` (in TF-3), `p #10 destroyer` (in TF-3).
                            *   So TF-3 controls the sea escort capability.
                            *   TF-1 only has armies. They cannot cross water on their own. They need a transport + warship to move armies from Land (5,2) to East of enemy? Or can they move from (5,2) to somewhere safe and ferry?
                            *   Wait, the prompt says: "An army can never enter water. Transports carry them...".
                            *   So TF-1 must load onto a transport. But who loads them? TF-3 is Patroling. Can I transfer control? Or send TF-3 to move?
                            *   Actually, I can order TF-3 to STAGE a transport with an army. But TF-3 is PATROL (7,2). Its objective is to screen.
                            *   The "ORDERS CONTRACT" allows `FORM TF ... UNITS`.
                            *   Or `REINFORCE` or `DISBAND`.
                            *   TF-1 has armies at (5,x). I should consolidate them if possible. But they are already in a group? "STAGE (5,2)".
                            *   Wait, TF-3 is on the patrol. Enemy destroyer at (8,3) seen t49. Transport #9 is with TF-3.
                            *   If I want to move armies East from (5,x) to enemy (11,x). The map shows `~` between 5 and 11?
                            *   Let's check the water extent. r0 `c ~ ~`. c is at col 4/5. Then water starts.
                            *   To cross from Land side to Enemy side: Need Transport.
                            *   Transport #9 is with TF-3. It is empty (according to unit list). Why? TF-3 says "screen the crossing lane". Maybe it's waiting for an attack vector.
                            *   If I make TF-1 carry armies, they need a transport assigned to them OR I must create a new Task Force for the Transport.
                            *   Actually, `TF <id>: ...`. I can order TF-3 to change objective? "RETASK".
                            *   Or I can `FORM` a new TF with the transport and armies from TF-1. But TF-1's units are land-only.
                            *   Wait, I have a constraint: "Every standing TF gets exactly one line... a TF may receive both a REINFORCE line and a RETASK line".
                            *   If I want to move the armies from (5,2) East across water using Transport #16 (which is at 1,2 in a city).
                            *   Wait, Transport #16 is at (1,2). This is West of TF-1's current staging area (5,2)? Or are these x coordinates? Yes. 1 < 5.
                            *   Enemy cities are at x=11.
                            *   So Transport #16 is further West. I need to move armies East.
                            *   Where can the transport go? A city builds a unit. Transport can be built/moved between friendly cities or coast.
                            *   My cities: (2,0), (1,2), (4,3), (4,1).
                            *   City (1,2) has Transport #16. City (2,0) has Army.
                            *   I need to move armies to East Coast (x > water start?). Enemy at x=11. Water starts somewhere between 5 and 11 based on `~`.
                            *   Wait, r4 has `m h . ~`. m is army #17? Yes. h is army #8.
                            *   Map shows armies are already in water territory (r2c5 is e).
                            *   If I am at (5,2), and enemies are at (11,1)/(11,2).
                            *   I have Transport #9 with TF-3. But TF-3 has Destroyer.
                            *   Can I use TF-3 to transport TF-1 armies?
                            *   Constraint: "A warship grouped with a transport escorts it". TF-3 already has `n` (#9) and `p` (#10 destroyer).
                            *   So TF-3 is the Sea Task Force.
                            *   I can order TF-3 to take on the objective of moving the troops?
                            *   BUT, Army units are "Land". They cannot enter water. They must be in a transport.
                            *   "Transports carry them: a transport loads up...".
                            *   This implies I need to issue orders that load them. But Officers handle loading.
                            *   If I RETASK TF-3 to CAPTURE (11,1), the officers will try to move it there. It has a transport and destroyer. They can sail. But they don't have armies loaded yet.
                            *   Can I transfer armies from TF-1 to TF-3? No direct command for "Transfer Units".
                            *   I can `REINFORCE` a Task Force. "TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids> | <one line>". This adds unassigned units to a TF and keeps the objective.
                            *   But TF-1 units are ASSIGNED to TF-1. They aren't UNASSIGNED.
                            *   Can I DISBAND TF-1, FORM new with Transport? Yes, that works.
                            *   Better strategy: TF-3 is already set up (Transport + Destroyer). Its tasking is PATROL (7,2). It is on station. Enemy Destr at (8,3). It should protect itself and escort the transport across.
                            *   If I change TF-3's objective to CAPTURE (11,1), it will sail.
                            *   But where are the armies? They are in TF-1.
                            *   Wait, "TF <id>: REINFORCE UNITS <ids>". If I reinforce TF-1 with units from TF-3? No, TF-3 is a TF.
                            *   "UNASSIGNED units enter play only through FORM or REINFORCE". TF-1 armies are assigned to TF-1.
                            *   Can I move units between TFs without DISBAND/FORM?
                            *   The prompt doesn't explicitly allow unit transfer. It implies Officers handle movement. But you can't say "Move army c from TF-1 to TF-3".
                            *   Maybe the intention is for TF-1 to load onto Transport #16 (at 1,2). But Transport #16 is West of TF-1? No. (1,2) vs (5,2).
                            *   Ah, maybe I can order TF-1 to STAGE at (4,3)? City (4,3) building Army. Can they load there?
                            *   Let's look at the Transport #16 status: "(1,2) in city, empty".
                            *   Maybe I need to move it closer to 5,2 or use it?
